classifications.phrase: inflation continues to moderate, but is declining more slowly than expected
services inflation, which remains high and is moderating only gradually
continuing excess demand in the economy, coupled with strong domestic cost pressures
Conditions in the labour market have eased over the past year, but remain tighter than…
Wages growth appears to have peaked but is still above the level that can be…
output growth has been subdued, reflecting weak household consumption growth
The persistence of services inflation is a key uncertainty
Growth in unit labour costs also remains very high
household consumption growth has been particularly weak
there is a risk that household consumption picks up more slowly than expected, resulting in…
Returning inflation to target within a reasonable timeframe remains the Boards highest priority
inflation is easing, it is doing so more slowly than previously expected and it remains…
will remain vigilant to upside risks
The Board remains resolute in its determination to return inflation to target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance on inflation (slow moderation, services persistence, wage pressures, excess demand) balanced against dovish growth concerns (weak consumption, subdued output), while maintaining a hold on rates pending further data.
